=============================================================================\n952\t// INTEGRATION TESTS\n953\t// =============================================================================\n954\t\n955\tfunc TestFormattingIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n956\t\t// Test that formatting functions work well together\n957\t\terr := ValidationError{\n958\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n959\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n960\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n961\t\t\tExpected:  \"value\",\n962\t\t\tActual:    nil,\n963\t\t\tLocation:  \"line 5\",\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\t// Format with severity\n967\t\tfullMsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n968\t\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"High\") {\n969\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected severity in formatted error\")\n970\t\t}\n971\t\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"required\") {\n972\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error type in formatted error\")\n973\t\t}\n974\t\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"email\") {\n975\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in formatted error\")\n976\t\t}\n977\t\n978\t\t// Format brief\n979\t\tbriefMsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n980\t\tif !strings.Contains(briefMsg, \"email\") {\n981\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in brief error\")\n982\t\t}\n983\t\tif tt.want)\n1070\t\t\t\t}\n1071\t\t\t})\n1072\t\t}\n1073\t}\n1074\t\n1075\tfunc TestFormatErrorBackwardCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n1076\t\t// Test that string-based FormatError produces consistent results\n1077\t\t// with ErrorType-based FormatErrorWithType\n1078\t\tt.Run(\"string and ErrorType functions produce consistent results\", func(t *testing.T) {\n1079\t\t\tmessage := \"Field is required\"\n1080\t\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n1081\t\n1082\t\t\t// String-based function\n1083\t\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"required\", message, fieldName)\n1084\t\n1085\t\t\t// ErrorType-based function\n1086\t\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRequired, message, fieldName)\n1087\t\n1088\t\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n1089\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String and ErrorType functions should produce same result:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n1090\t\t\t}\n1091\t\t})\n1092\t\n1093\t\tt.Run(\"format error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) FormatError(ErrTypeFormat, message, fieldName)\n1099\t\n1100\t\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n1101\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n1102\t\t\t}\n1103\t\t})\n1104\t\n1105\t\tt.Run(\"range error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n1106\t\t\tmessage := \"Value out of range\"\n1107\t\t\tfieldName :=   string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"field with parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"request\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"field without parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty field name with parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"request\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty field name without parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"(unknown field)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"nested field\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"address.city\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"user\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"user.address.city\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"whitespace handling\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"  email  \",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"  request  \",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index in field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index without parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index with bracket notation\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"data\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"data.users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"multiple array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"data.0.items.1.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.data[0].items[1].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"parent with array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"both parent and field with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"profile.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0].profile.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"array index at start of field\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"0.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users.0.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty field name with array parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"users.0\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[0]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"deeply nested with array indices\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"items.0.data.values.1.field\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.items[0].data.values[1].field\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"negative array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.-1.email\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"response\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"response.users[-1].email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"large array index\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"users.12345.name\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"users[12345].name\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldRef(tt.fieldName, tt.parent)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldRef() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatFieldLocationWith(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\tlocation  string\n\t\tparent    string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"field with location\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tlocation:  \"line 5\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"request\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request.email at line 5\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"field without location\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tlocation:  \"\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"request\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"request.email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"field with location no parent\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\tlocation:  \"position 123\",\n\t\t\tparent:    \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"email at position 123\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldLocationWith(tt.fieldName, tt.location, tt.parent)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldLocationWith() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatFieldListWith(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tfields      []string\n\t\tconjunction string\n\t\texpected    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"empty list\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"and\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"single field\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{\"email\"},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"and\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"two fields\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{\"email\", \"password\"},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"and\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email and password\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"three fields\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"and\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email, password, and name\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"four fields\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\", \"age\"},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"and\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email, password, name, and age\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"or conjunction\",\n\t\t\tfields:      []string{\"email\", \"phone\"},\n\t\t\tconjunction: \"or\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email or phone\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldListWith(tt.fields, tt.conjunction)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldListWith() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorMessage(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\texpected  string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"with all components\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"without field name\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"format\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid email format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[format] Invalid email format\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"without error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Some error occurred\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"email: Some error occurred\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"message only\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Some error occurred\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"Some error occurred\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"whitespace handling\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"  required  \",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"  Field is required  \",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"  email  \",\n\t\t\texpected:  \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorMessage(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorMessage() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorWithValues(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname          string\n\t\tfieldName     string\n\t\texpected      interface{}\n\t\tactual        interface{}\n\t\tcustomMessage string\n\t\texpectedMsg   string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"with field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:     \"status_code\",\n\t\t\texpected:      200,\n\t\t\tactual:        404,\n\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"with custom message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:     \"count\",\n\t\t\texpected:      5,\n\t\t\tactual:        3,\n\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"Not enough items\",\n\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"without field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:     \"\",\n\t\t\texpected:      100,\n\t\t\tactual:        50,\n\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"expected 100, got 50\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:          \"string values\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:     \"type\",\n\t\t\texpected:      \"number\",\n\t\t\tactual:        \"string\",\n\t\t\tcustomMessage: \"\",\n\t\t\texpectedMsg:   \"type: expected number, got string\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithValues(tt.fieldName, tt.expected, tt.actual, tt.customMessage)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expectedMsg {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorWithValues() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expectedMsg)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorWithRange(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tfieldName   string\n\t\tminValue   interface{}\n\t\tmaxValue   interface{}\n\t\tactualValue interface{}\n\t\texpected    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"with field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"age\",\n\t\t\tminValue:    0,\n\t\t\tmaxValue:    120,\n\t\t\tactualValue: 150,\n\t\t\texpected:    \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"without field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"\",\n\t\t\tminValue:    1,\n\t\t\tmaxValue:    10,\n\t\t\tactualValue: 15,\n\t\t\texpected:    \"value 15 is outside valid range [1, 10]\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithRange(tt.fieldName, tt.minValue, tt.maxValue, tt.actualValue)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorWithRange() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorWithPattern(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname        string\n\t\tfieldName   string\n\t\tpattern     string\n\t\tactualValue interface{}\n\t\texpected    string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"with field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"email\",\n\t\t\tpattern:     \"must contain @\",\n\t\t\tactualValue: \"invalidemail\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:        \"without field name\",\n\t\t\tfieldName:   \"\",\n\t\t\tpattern:     \"^[a-z]+$\",\n\t\t\tactualValue: \"ABC123\",\n\t\t\texpected:    \"value 'ABC123' does not match pattern (^[a-z]+$)\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithPattern(tt.fieldName, tt.pattern, tt.actualValue)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorWithPattern() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestFormatErrorType(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\terrorType ErrorType\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"required\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Required Field\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"format\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeFormat,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Format Error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"range\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeRange,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Range Error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"length\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeLength,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Length Error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeType,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Type Error\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"value\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeValue,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Invalid Value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"duplicate\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Duplicate Value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"conflict\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeConflict,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Conflict\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"unknown\",\n\t\t\terrorType: ErrTypeUnknown,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Unknown Error\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorType(tt.errorType)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatErrorType() = %v, want %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorTypeFrom(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname \"medium\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityMedium,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Medium\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"low\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityLow,\n\t\t\texpected: \"Low\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: TestFormatSeverityWithIndicator(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tseverity ErrorSeverity\n\t\texpected string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"critical\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityCritical,\n\t\t\texpected: \"[!] CRITICAL\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"high\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityHigh,\n\t\t\texpected: \"[⚠] High\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"medium\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityMedium,\n\t\t\texpected: \"[■] Medium\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"low\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityLow,\n\t\t\texpected: \"[○] Low\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"info\",\n\t\t\tseverity: SeverityInfo,\n\t\t\texpected: \"[i] Info\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests %v\", result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatValidationErrorFull(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\terr             ValidationError\n\t\tincludeSeverity bool\n\t\texpected        string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with all fields and severity\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t\tExpected:  \"value\",\n\t\t\t\tActual:    nil,\n\t\t\t\tLocation:  \"line 5\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\texpected:        \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required (expected: value) [line 5]\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with severity\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\texpected:        \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"without severity\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Invalid format\",\n\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: false,\n\t\t\texpected:        \"[format] email: Invalid format\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with expected and actual\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: \"status_code\",\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Wrong status code\",\n\t\t\t\tFieldName: =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormattingIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that formatting functions work well together\n\terr := ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\tExpected:  \"value\",\n\t\tActual:    nil,\n\t\tLocation:  \"line 5\",\n\t}\n\n\t// Format with severity\n\tfullMsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"High\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected severity in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"required\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error type in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(fullMsg, \"email\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in formatted error\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Format brief\n\tbriefMsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n\tif !strings.Contains(briefMsg, \"email\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected field name in brief error\")\n\t}\n\tif !strings.Contains(briefMsg, \"required\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error type in brief error\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FORMATERROR FUNCTION TESTS (from format_helper.go)\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname      string\n\t\terrorType string\n\t\tmessage   string\n\t\tfieldName string\n\t\twant      string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"required error with field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"required\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[required] email: Field is required\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"format error without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"format\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Invalid email format\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[format] Invalid email format\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"range error with field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"range\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Value out of range\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"age\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[range] age: Value out of range\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"type error\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Wrong type\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"count\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[type] count: Wrong type\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty error type uses default\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Something went wrong\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] Something went wrong\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty message uses fallback with field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"validation\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[validation] email: email validation failed\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"empty message uses fallback without field\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[error] (no message provided)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"status_code error\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"status_code\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Expected 200 but got 404\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"response\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[status_code] response: Expected 200 but got 404\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:      \"unknown error type\",\n\t\t\terrorType: \"custom_type\",\n\t\t\tmessage:   \"Custom error message\",\n\t\t\tfieldName: \"field\",\n\t\t\twant:      \"[custom_type] field: Custom error message\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tgot := FormatErrorString(tt.errorType, tt.message, tt.fieldName)\n\t\t\tif got != tt.want {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatError() = %v, want %v\", got, tt.want)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorBackwardCompatibility(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that string-based FormatError produces consistent results\n\t// with ErrorType-based FormatErrorWithType\n\tt.Run(\"string and ErrorType functions produce consistent results\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Field is required\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\t// String-based function\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"required\", message, fieldName)\n\n\t\t// ErrorType-based function\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRequired, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"String and ErrorType functions should produce same result:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"format error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Invalid format\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"email\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"format\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeFormat, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"range error consistency\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tmessage := \"Value out of range\"\n\t\tfieldName := \"age\"\n\n\t\tstringResult := FormatErrorString(\"range\", message, fieldName)\n\t\ttypeResult := FormatError(ErrTypeRange, message, fieldName)\n\n\t\tif stringResult != typeResult {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Format inconsistency:\\n  String: %v\\n  Type:   %v\", stringResult, typeResult)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestFormatErrorConsistentClassification(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test that all ErrorType enum values are properly classified\n\tt.Run(\"all ErrorType values produce valid output\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tallTypes := []ErrorType{\n\t\t\tErrTypeRequired,\n\t\t\tErrTypeFormat,\n\t\t\tErrTypeRange,\n\t\t\tErrTypeLength,\n\t\t\tErrTypeType,\n\t\t\tErrTypeValue,\n\t\t\tErrTypeDuplicate,\n\t\t\tErrTypeConflict,\n\t\t\tErrTypeUnknown,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor _, et := range allTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorWithType(et, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, et.String()) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing %v, got %v\", et, et.String(), result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains field name\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"field\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing field name, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains message\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, \"Test message\") {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ErrorType %v should produce output containing message, got %v\", et, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"string-based error types are properly handled\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\terrorTypes := []string{\"required\", \"format\", \"range\", \"type\", \"unknown\"}\n\n\t\tfor _, etStr := range errorTypes {\n\t\t\tresult := FormatErrorString(etStr, \"Test message\", \"field\")\n\n\t\t\t// Check that result contains error type\n\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(result, etStr) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error type %v should produce output containing error type, got %v\", etStr, result)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CONTEXT FORMATTING TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\nfunc TestFormatValidationErrorFullWithContext(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname            string\n\t\terr             ValidationError\n\t\tincludeSeverity bool\n\t\tcontext         *ValidationErrorContext\n\t\texpected        string\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with full context (location and related fields)\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Field is required\",\n\t\t\t\tFieldName: \"email\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tincludeSeverity: true,\n\t\t\tcontext: func() *ValidationErrorContext {\n\t\t\t\tctx := ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 5\"}\n\t\t\t\tctx.RelatedFields = []string{\"email_confirmation\", \"user.email\"}\n\t\t\t\treturn &ctx\n\t\t\t}(),\n\t\t\texpected: \"[⚠️] High [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation and user.email)\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname: \"with location only\",\n\t\t\terr: ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\tErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n\t\t\t\tMessage:   \"Invalid formatting\n\tt.Run(\"HasLocation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tctx := ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 5\"}\n\t\tif !ctx.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected HasLocation to return true\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\temptyCtx := ValidationErrorContext{}\n\t\tif emptyCtx.HasLocation() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected HasLocation to return false for empty context\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"HasRelatedFields\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tctx := ValidationErrorContext{RelatedFields: []string{\"field1\", \"field2\"}}\n\t\tif !ctx.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected HasRelatedFields to return true\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\temptyCtx := ValidationErrorContext{}\n\t\tif emptyCtx.HasRelatedFields() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected HasRelatedFields to return false for empty context\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"IsEmpty\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tctx1 := ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 5\"}\n\t\tif ctx1.IsEmpty() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsEmpty to return false for context with location\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx2 := ValidationErrorContext{RelatedFields: []string{\"field1\"}}\n\t\tif ctx2.IsEmpty() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsEmpty to return false for context with related fields\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\temptyCtx := ValidationErrorContext{}\n\t\tif !emptyCtx.IsEmpty() {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected IsEmpty to return true for empty context\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"String representation\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tctx := ValidationErrorContext{Location: \"line 5\"}\n\t\tctx.RelatedFields = []string{\"field1\", \"field2\"}\n\t\tstr := ctx.String()\n\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(str, \"location: line 5\") {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"String representation should contain location\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(str, \"related_fields:\") {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"String representation should contain related_fields\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// \"empty suggestions\",\n\t\t\tsuggestions:       []string{},\n\t\t\texpectedContains:  []string{},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\terr := {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_4370e2c2ed39476e9fe4301e","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tpackage validate\n2\t\n3\timport (\n4\t\t\"fmt\"\n5\t\t\"strings\"\n6\t)\n7\t\n8\t/*\n9\tError Formatting Helpers\n10\t\n11\tThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\n12\tThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\n13\tto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n14\t*/\n15\t\n16\t// =============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error email format\", \"\")\n171\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n172\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n173\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\t// Uses emoji indicators for clear visual distinction:\n453\t// - 🚨 (Critical): Alert/ sirens for critical issues\n454\t// - ⚠️ (High): Warning sign for high severity\n455\t// - ⚡ (Medium): Lightning bolt for medium severity\n456\t// - ℹ️ (Low): Info for low severity issues\n457\t// - 💡 (Info): Light bulb for informational messages\n458\t// - ❓ (Unknown): Question mark for unknown severity\n459\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n460\t\tswitch severity {\n461\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n462\t\t\treturn \"🚨\"\n463\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n464\t\t\treturn \"⚠️\"\n465\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n466\t\t\treturn \"⚡\"\n467\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n468\t\t\treturn \"ℹ️\"\n469\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n470\t\t\treturn \"💡\"\n471\t\tdefault:\n472\t\t\treturn \"❓\"\n473\t\t}\n474\t}\n475\t\n476\t// =============================================================================\n477\t// EXPECTED VS ACTUAL FORMATTING\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// FormatExpectedActual formats an ExpectedActual struct into a readable string.\n481\t// This function handles different value types and formats them side-by-side.\n482\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty (both values nil).\n483\t//\n484\t// Parameters:\n485\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n486\t//\n487\t// Returns a formatted string showing expected vs actual values, or empty string if nil/empty.\n488\t//\n489\t// Example usage:\n490\t//\n491\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n492\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n493\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found)\"\n494\t//\n495\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(\"test@example.com\", \"invalid\")\n496\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActual(ea)\n497\t//\t// Returns: \"expected: 'test@example.com', actual: 'invalid'\"\n498\tfunc FormatExpectedActual(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n499\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n500\t\t\treturn \"\"\n501\t\t}\n502\t\n503\t\tvar expectedStr, actualStr string\n504\t\n505\t\t// Format expected value\n506\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n507\t\t\tswitch exp := ea.Expected.(type) {\n508\t\t\tcase int:\n509\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", exp, getStatusCodeDescription(exp))\n510\t\t\tcase []int:\n511\t\t\t\tparts := make([]string, len(exp))\n512\t\t\t\tfor i, code := range exp {\n513\t\t\t\t\tparts[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", code, getStatusCodeDescription(code))\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"one of [%s]\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n516\t\t\tcase string:\n517\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", exp)\n518\t\t\tcase float64:\n519\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", exp)\n520\t\t\tcase []string:\n521\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(exp))\n522\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range exp {\n523\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n524\t\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n526\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n527\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatMapValue(exp)\n528\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n529\t\t\t\texpectedStr = formatSliceValue(exp)\n530\t\t\tdefault:\n531\t\t\t\texpectedStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", exp)\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\t}\n534\t\n535\t\t// Format actual value\n536\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n537\t\t\tswitch act := ea.Actual.(type) {\n538\t\t\tcase int:\n539\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%d (%s)\", act, getStatusCodeDescription(act))\n540\t\t\tcase string:\n541\t\t\t\t// Truncate long strings\n542\t\t\t\tif len(act) > 100 {\n543\t\t\t\t\tact = act[:100] + \"...\"\n544\t\t\t\t}\n545\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", act)\n546\t\t\tcase float64:\n547\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%.2f\", act)\n548\t\t\tcase []string:\n549\t\t\t\tquoted := make([]string, len(act))\n550\t\t\t\tfor i, s := range act {\n551\t\t\t\t\tquoted[i] = fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", s)\n552\t\t\t\t}\n553\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(quoted, \", \"))\n554\t\t\tcase map[string]interface{}:\n555\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatMapValue(act)\n556\t\t\tcase []interface{}:\n557\t\t\t\tactualStr = formatSliceValue(act)\n558\t\t\tdefault:\n559\t\t\t\tactualStr = fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", act)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\n563\t\t// Build the formatted string\n564\t\tvar parts []string\n565\t\tif expectedStr != \"\" {\n566\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", expectedStr))\n567\t\t}\n568\t\tif actualStr != \"\" {\n569\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", actualStr))\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n573\t\t\treturn \"\"\n574\t\t}\n575\t\n576\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n577\t}\n578\t\n579\t// formatMapValue formats a map value for display in error messages.\n580\tfunc formatMapValue(m map[string]interface{}) string {\n581\t\tif len(m) == 0 {\n582\t\t\treturn \"{}\"\n583\t\t}\n584\t\n585\t\tvar pairs []string\n586\t\tfor k, v := range m {\n587\t\t\tpairs = append(pairs, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", k, v))\n588\t\t}\n589\t\n590\t\t// Limit output for large maps\n591\t\tif len(pairs) > 5 {\n592\t\t\tpairs = pairs[:5]\n593\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s, ...}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"{%s}\", strings.Join(pairs, \", \"))\n597\t}\n598\t\n599\t// formatSliceValue formats a slice value for display in error messages.\n600\tfunc formatSliceValue(s []interface{}) string {\n601\t\tif len(s) == 0 {\n602\t\t\treturn \"[]\"\n603\t\t}\n604\t\n605\t\tvar items []string\n606\t\tfor _, v := range s {\n607\t\t\titems = append(items, fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v))\n608\t\t}\n609\t\n610\t\t// Limit output for large slices\n611\t\tif len(items) > 5 {\n612\t\t\titems = items[:5]\n613\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s, ...]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n614\t\t}\n615\t\n616\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s]\", strings.Join(items, \", \"))\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// FormatExpectedActualInline formats ExpectedActual values inline (compact format).\n620\t// This is useful for embedding in error messages where space is limited.\n621\t// Returns empty string if the ExpectedActual is empty.\n622\t//\n623\t// Parameters:\n624\t//   - ea: The ExpectedActual struct to format\n625\t//\n626\t// Returns a compact formatted string.\n627\t//\n628\t// Example usage:\n629\t//\n630\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n631\t//\tformatted := FormatExpectedActualInline(ea)\n632\t//\t// Returns: \"(expected 200, got 404)\"\n633\tfunc FormatExpectedActualInline(ea ExpectedActual) string {\n634\t\tif ea.IsEmpty() {\n635\t\t\treturn \"\"\n636\t\t}\n637\t\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tif ea.HasExpected() {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v\", ea.Expected))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\tif ea.HasActual() {\n643\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"got %v\", ea.Actual))\n644\t\t}\n645\t\n646\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n647\t\t\treturn \"\"\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"(%s)\", strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// =============================================================================\n654\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n655\t// =============================================================================\n656\t\n657\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n658\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n659\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n660\t//\n661\t// Parameters:\n662\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n663\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n664\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n665\t//\n666\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n667\t//\n668\t// Example usage:\n669\t//\n670\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n671\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n672\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n673\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n674\t//\t}\n675\t//\n676\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, nil)\n677\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n678\t//\n679\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n680\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true, ctx)\n681\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n682\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n683\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n684\t\n685\t\t// Add severity if requested\n686\t\tif includeSeverity {\n687\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n688\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n689\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n690\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n691\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n692\t\t\t} else {\n693\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n694\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n695\t\t\t}\n696\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n697\t\t}\n698\t\n699\t\t// Add error type and field\n700\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n701\t\n702\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n703\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n704\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n705\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n706\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n707\t\t\t}\n708\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n709\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n710\t\t\t}\n711\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n712\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n713\t\t\t}\n714\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n715\t\t}\n716\t\n717\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n718\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n719\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n723\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n724\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n725\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n728\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\n731\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n732\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n733\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n737\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n738\t\t}\n739\t\n740\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n741\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n742\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n743\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n744\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n745\t\t\t}\n746\t\t}\n747\t\n748\t\treturn builder.String()\n749\t}\n750\t\n751\t// FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual creates a comprehensive, formatted error message\n752\t// with optional ExpectedActual parameter for value comparison. This extends FormatValidationErrorFull\n753\t// to support structured expected vs actual value display.\n754\t//\n755\t// Parameters:\n756\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n757\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n758\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n759\t//   - expectedActual: Optional ExpectedActual for structured value comparison display\n760\t//\n761\t// Returns a formatted error message string with optional expected/actual comparison.\n762\t//\n763\t// The ExpectedActual parameter takes precedence over err.Expected/err.Actual when provided.\n764\t// If expectedActual is nil or empty, the function falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual.\n765\t// If both are empty, no value comparison is included.\n766\t//\n767\t// Example usage:\n768\t//\n769\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n770\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n771\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n772\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n773\t//\t}\n774\t//\n775\t//\t// With ExpectedActual parameter\n776\t//\tea := NewExpectedActual(200, 404)\n777\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ea)\n778\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (expected: 200 (OK), actual: 404 (Not Found))\"\n779\t//\n780\t//\t// Without ExpectedActual parameter (falls back to err.Expected/err.Actual)\n781\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err, true, nil, ExpectedActual{})\n782\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n783\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithExpectedActual(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext, expectedActual ExpectedActual) string {\n784\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n785\t\n786\t\t// Add severity if requested\n787\t\tif includeSeverity {\n788\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n789\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n790\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n791\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n792\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n793\t\t\t} else {\n794\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n795\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n796\t\t\t}\n797\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n798\t\t}\n799\t\n800\t\t// Add error type and field\n801\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n802\t\n803\t\t// Add expected/actual values - prefer ExpectedActual parameter if provided and non-empty\n804\t\tif !expectedActual.IsEmpty() {\n805\t\t\tformattedEA := FormatExpectedActual(expectedActual)\n806\t\t\tif formattedEA != \"\" {\n807\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (%s)\", formattedEA))\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\t} else if err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n810\t\t\t// Fall back to err.Expected/err.Actual\n811\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n812\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n813\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n814\t\t\t}\n815\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n816\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n817\t\t\t}\n818\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n819\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n820\t\t\t}\n821\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n822\t\t}\n823\t\n824\t\t// Add location if present in the error itself\n825\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n826\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n827\t\t}\n828\t\n829\t\t// Add context information if provided and not empty\n830\t\tif context != nil && !context.IsEmpty() {\n831\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n832\t\t\tvar contextParts []string\n833\t\n834\t\t\tif context.HasLocation() {\n835\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", context.Location))\n836\t\t\t}\n837\t\n838\t\t\tif context.HasRelatedFields() {\n839\t\t\t\tfieldsList := FormatFieldList(context.RelatedFields)\n840\t\t\t\tcontextParts = append(contextParts, fmt.Sprintf(\"related fields: %s\", fieldsList))\n841\t\t\t}\n842\t\n843\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(strings.Join(contextParts, \", \"))\n844\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n845\t\t}\n846\t\n847\t\t// Add suggestions if present\n848\t\tif len(err.Suggestions) > 0 {\n849\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\"\\nSuggestions:\")\n850\t\t\tfor _, suggestion := range err.Suggestions {\n851\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n  - %s\", suggestion))\n852\t\t\t}\n853\t\t}\n854\t\n855\t\treturn builder.String()\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n859\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n860\t//\n861\t// Parameters:\n862\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n863\t//\n864\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n865\t//\n866\t// Example usage:\n867\t//\n868\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n869\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n870\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n871\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n872\t//\t}\n873\t//\n874\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n875\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n876\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n877\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n878\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n879\t\t}\n880\t\n881\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n882\t}\n883\t\n884\t// =============================================================================\n885\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n886\t// =============================================================================\n887\t\n888\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n889\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n890\t//\n891\t// Parameters:\n892\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n893\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n894\t//\n895\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n896\t//\n897\t// Example usage:\n898\t//\n899\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n900\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n901\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n902\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n903\t//\t}, {\n904\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n905\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n906\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n907\t//\t}}\n908\t//\n909\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n910\t//\t// Returns:\n911\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n912\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n913\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n914\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n915\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n916\t\t}\n917\t\n918\t\tvar lines []string\n919\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n920\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n921\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n922\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n923\t\t}\n924\t\n925\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n926\t}\n927\t\n928\t// FormatErrorListWithContext formats multiple errors into a readable list with context information.\n929\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures with additional context for each error.\n930\t//\n931\t// Parameters:\n932\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n933\t//   - contexts: Slice of ValidationErrorContext structs, one per error (can contain nil values)\n934\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n935\t//\n936\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n937\t//\n938\t// If contexts is nil or shorter than errors, missing contexts are treated as nil (no context).\n939\t//\n940\t// Example usage:\n941\t//\n942\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n943\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n944\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n945\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n946\t//\t}, {\n947\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n948\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n949\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n950\t//\t}}\n951\t//\n952\t//\tcontexts := []*ValidationErrorContext{\n953\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"}),\n954\t//\t    NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 10\"),\n955\t//\t}\n956\t//\n957\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorListWithContext(errors, contexts, true)\n958\t//\t// Returns:\n959\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\n960\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format (location: line 10)\n961\tfunc FormatErrorListWithContext(errors []ValidationError, contexts []*ValidationErrorContext, includeSeverity bool) string {\n962\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n963\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\tvar lines []string\n967\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n968\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n969\t\n970\t\t\tvar context *ValidationErrorContext\n971\t\t\tif contexts != nil && i < len(contexts) {\n972\t\t\t\tcontext = contexts[i]\n973\t\t\t}\n974\t\n975\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n976\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n977\t\t}\n978\t\n979\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n980\t}\n981\t\n982\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n983\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n984\t//\n985\t// Parameters:\n986\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n987\t//\n988\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n989\t//\n990\t// Example usage:\n991\t//\n992\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n993\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n994\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n995\t//\t}, {\n996\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n997\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n998\t//\t}}\n999\t//\n1000\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n1001\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n1002\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n1003\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1004\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1005\t\t}\n1006\t\n1007\t\t// Count errors by type\n1008\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n1009\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n1010\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n1011\t\t}\n1012\t\n1013\t\t// Build summary parts\n1014\t\tvar parts []string\n1015\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n1016\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n1017\t\t}\n1018\t\n1019\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n1020\t}\n1021\t\n1022\t// =============================================================================\n1023\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n1024\t// =============================================================================\n1025\t\n1026\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n1027\t// without the parent parameter.\n1028\t//\n1029\t// Parameters:\n1030\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n1031\t//   - location: Additional location context\n1032\t//\n1033\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n1034\t//\n1035\t// Example usage:\n1036\t//\n1037\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n1038\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n1039\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n1040\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n1041\t}\n1042\t\n1043\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n1044\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n1045\t//\n1046\t// Parameters:\n1047\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n1048\t//\n1049\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n1050\t//\n1051\t// Example usage:\n1052\t//\n1053\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n1054\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n1055\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n1056\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n1057\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull without context.\n1060\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n1061\t//\n1062\t// Parameters:\n1063\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1064\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1065\t//\n1066\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1067\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n1068\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, nil)\n1069\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t// FormatValidationErrorWithContext creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError\n1072\t// with optional context information. This is a convenience function that simplifies the common case\n1073\t// of formatting an error with context.\n1074\t//\n1075\t// Parameters:\n1076\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n1077\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n1078\t//   - context: Optional ValidationErrorContext for additional location and field information\n1079\t//\n1080\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n1081\t//\n1082\t// Example usage:\n1083\t//\n1084\t//\tctx := NewValidationErrorContext(\"line 5\").WithRelatedFields([]string{\"email_confirmation\"})\n1085\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err, true, ctx)\n1086\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required (location: line 5, related fields: email_confirmation)\"\n1087\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithContext(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool, context *ValidationErrorContext) string {\n1088\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity, context)\n1089\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n1092\t//\n1093\t// Parameters:\n1094\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n1095\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n1096\t//\n1097\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n1098\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n1099\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n1100\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t// =============================================================================\n1103\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n1104\t// =============================================================================\n1105\t\n1106\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n1107\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n1108\t//\n1109\t// Parameters:\n1110\t//   - value: The value to quote\n1111\t//\n1112\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n1113\t//\n1114\t// Example
